# KNIGHT SESSION 24 — Wire the Dispatch Pipeline + Print Orders + Canonical Migration

## Generated by: Bishop (Claude Desktop) — Bishop008 Session, 15 March 2026
## Priority: HIGH — Everything here is "last mile" wiring that makes existing systems actually fire

---

## CONTEXT

Sessions 22-23 built the infrastructure. Session 24 wires it together.

**What exists (built but not connected):**
- `outboundDispatch.ts` — Full approval workflow (draft → review → stamped → queued → dispatched). "As You Wish" stamp mechanism. 9 content types, 10 dispatch channels. Session 7E launch queue pre-loaded with 13 items.
- `UniversalDispatch.tsx` — UI for creating content, selecting targets, previewing, scheduling. But `handleDispatch()` just calls `onDispatch` callback — no actual sending.
- `contentPipeline.ts` — 5-stage content evolution (seed → paper). DB table exists. `battery_campaign_id` and `cue_card_id` fields ready.
- `TheBattery.tsx` — 3 pre-loaded campaigns. Three-tier safety (SAFE → ARMED → FIRING). But fires into `process-scheduled-posts` which only handles Twitter/LinkedIn/Facebook.
- `send-transactional-email` edge function — 6 email types including `outreach` (added Session 23). Live and tested.
- `printful-api` edge function — Live, returns 200. Catalog browse, estimates, draft/confirmed orders.
- `ipfs-pin` edge function — Live, real CIDs on Pinata gateway.
- `process-scheduled-posts` edge function — 5-minute cron. Posts to Twitter, LinkedIn, Facebook only.
- `print_orders` + `print_batches` tables — Created Session 22. Founder approval gate pattern.

**What's NOT connected (Session 24 scope):**
1. Outbound Dispatch → actual edge function calls (email, medium, social)
2. Medium API integration (no edge function exists)
3. Pre-loading the dispatch queue with real items (Dead Internet article, Moo outreach, CFA outreach)
4. Print order service wiring (deferred from Session 23)
5. useCanonicalStats() component migration (deferred from Session 23)

## TASK 1: Dispatch Executor Edge Function

Create a single edge function that receives a dispatch request and routes to the correct channel.

File: `platform/supabase/functions/dispatch-executor/index.ts`

```typescript
import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.168.0/http/server.ts";
import { createClient } from "https://esm.sh/@supabase/supabase-js@2";

const corsHeaders = {
  'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*',
  'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'authorization, x-client-info, apikey, content-type',
};

serve(async (req) => {
  if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') {
    return new Response(null, { headers: corsHeaders });
  }

  const supabaseUrl = Deno.env.get('SUPABASE_URL')!;
  const serviceKey = Deno.env.get('SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY')!;
  const supabase = createClient(supabaseUrl, serviceKey);

  try {
    const { channel, payload } = await req.json();
    // payload = { title, content, recipientEmail, recipientName, imageUrls, scheduledFor, outboundItemId }

    let result: any;

    switch (channel) {
      case 'email': {
        // Route through send-transactional-email with outreach type
        const emailRes = await fetch(`${supabaseUrl}/functions/v1/send-transactional-email`, {
          method: 'POST',
          headers: {
            'Authorization': `Bearer ${serviceKey}`,
            'Content-Type': 'application/json',
          },
          body: JSON.stringify({
            type: 'outreach',
            to: payload.recipientEmail,
            data: {
              recipientName: payload.recipientName,
              senderName: 'Liana Banyan Corporation',
              subject: payload.title,
              body: payload.content,
              ctaText: payload.ctaText || 'Learn More',
              ctaUrl: payload.ctaUrl || 'https://lianabanyan.com',
              cueCardType: payload.cueCardType || null,
            },
          }),
        });
        result = await emailRes.json();
        break;
      }

      case 'medium': {
        // Route through medium-publish edge function
        const mediumRes = await fetch(`${supabaseUrl}/functions/v1/medium-publish`, {
          method: 'POST',
          headers: {
            'Authorization': `Bearer ${serviceKey}`,
            'Content-Type': 'application/json',
          },
          body: JSON.stringify({
            title: payload.title,
            content: payload.content,
            tags: payload.tags || [],
            publishStatus: payload.publishStatus || 'draft',
          }),
        });
        result = await mediumRes.json();
        break;
      }

      case 'twitter':
      case 'linkedin':
      case 'facebook':
      case 'bluesky':
      case 'threads': {
        // Insert into scheduled_posts for the cron to pick up
        const { data, error } = await supabase
          .from('scheduled_posts')
          .insert({
            platform: channel,
            content: payload.content,
            title: payload.title,
            image_urls: payload.imageUrls || [],
            scheduled_for: payload.scheduledFor || new Date().toISOString(),
            status: 'pending',
          })
          .select()
          .single();
        if (error) throw error;
        result = data;
        break;
      }

      case 'cephas': {
        // Cephas is deployed via Hugo build — flag content for next deploy
        result = { status: 'flagged_for_deploy', note: 'Content will appear on next Hugo build + deploy' };
        break;
      }

      default:
        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({ error: `Unsupported channel: ${channel}` }),
          { status: 400, headers: { ...corsHeaders, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } }
        );
    }

    // Update outbound item status to 'dispatched' if outboundItemId provided
    if (payload.outboundItemId) {
      await supabase
        .from('outbound_dispatch')
        .update({
          status: 'dispatched',
          dispatched_at: new Date().toISOString(),
        })
        .eq('id', payload.outboundItemId);
    }

    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ success: true, channel, result }),
      { headers: { ...corsHeaders, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } }
    );
  } catch (err) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ error: err.message }),
      { status: 500, headers: { ...corsHeaders, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } }
    );
  }
});
```

Deploy: `npx supabase functions deploy dispatch-executor --project-ref <ref>`

## TASK 2: Medium Publishing Edge Function

Medium's API uses integration tokens. The Founder will need to create one at https://medium.com/me/settings/security (or we use their API keys if already in DoubleSecret.env).

File: `platform/supabase/functions/medium-publish/index.ts`

```typescript
import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.168.0/http/server.ts";

const corsHeaders = {
  'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*',
  'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'authorization, x-client-info, apikey, content-type',
};

serve(async (req) => {
  if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') {
    return new Response(null, { headers: corsHeaders });
  }

  const token = Deno.env.get('MEDIUM_INTEGRATION_TOKEN');
  if (!token) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ error: 'Medium not configured. Set MEDIUM_INTEGRATION_TOKEN in Supabase secrets.' }),
      { status: 503, headers: { ...corsHeaders, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } }
    );
  }

  try {
    const { title, content, tags, publishStatus } = await req.json();
    // publishStatus: 'public' | 'draft' | 'unlisted'

    // Step 1: Get authenticated user ID
    const meRes = await fetch('https://api.medium.com/v1/me', {
      headers: { 'Authorization': `Bearer ${token}`, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    });
    const me = await meRes.json();
    const userId = me.data.id;

    // Step 2: Create post
    const postRes = await fetch(`https://api.medium.com/v1/users/${userId}/posts`, {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: {
        'Authorization': `Bearer ${token}`,
        'Content-Type': 'application/json',
      },
      body: JSON.stringify({
        title,
        contentFormat: 'markdown',
        content,
        tags: tags?.slice(0, 5) || [],  // Medium allows max 5 tags
        publishStatus: publishStatus || 'draft',
        // canonicalUrl can point to Cephas version
        canonicalUrl: null,
      }),
    });

    const post = await postRes.json();
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify(post),
      { headers: { ...corsHeaders, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } }
    );
  } catch (err) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ error: err.message }),
      { status: 500, headers: { ...corsHeaders, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } }
    );
  }
});
```

Deploy: `npx supabase functions deploy medium-publish --project-ref <ref>`

**IMPORTANT**: The Founder needs to:
1. Go to https://medium.com/me/settings/security
2. Get an Integration Token
3. Run: `npx supabase secrets set MEDIUM_INTEGRATION_TOKEN="<token>" --project-ref <ref>`
   Or store in DoubleSecret.env and set from there.

## TASK 3: Wire UniversalDispatch to dispatch-executor

Currently `handleDispatch()` in `UniversalDispatch.tsx` just calls an optional callback. Wire it to actually call the edge function.

In `UniversalDispatch.tsx`, replace the `handleDispatch` function:

```typescript
const handleDispatch = async () => {
  const item: DispatchItem = {
    id: crypto.randomUUID(),
    content,
    title: title || undefined,
    imageUrls: initialImages,
    targets: selectedTargets,
    scheduledFor: scheduledFor || undefined,
    status: scheduledFor ? 'scheduled' : 'ready',
    createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
  };

  // Dispatch to each selected target via edge function
  for (const targetId of selectedTargets) {
    const channelMap: Record<string, string> = {
      'twitter': 'twitter',
      'linkedin': 'linkedin',
      'bluesky': 'bluesky',
      'threads': 'threads',
      'facebook': 'facebook',
      'medium': 'medium',
      'email': 'email',
      'discord': 'discord',
    };

    const channel = channelMap[targetId];
    if (channel) {
      try {
        const { data, error } = await supabase.functions.invoke('dispatch-executor', {
          body: {
            channel,
            payload: {
              title,
              content,
              imageUrls: initialImages,
              scheduledFor: scheduledFor || undefined,
              tags: [], // Can be populated from UI
            },
          },
        });
        if (error) console.error(`Dispatch to ${targetId} failed:`, error);
      } catch (err) {
        console.error(`Dispatch to ${targetId} error:`, err);
      }
    }
  }

  item.status = 'dispatched';
  onDispatch?.(item);
};
```

Add the supabase import at the top:
```typescript
import { supabase } from '@/integrations/supabase/client';
```

## TASK 4: Outbound Dispatch DB Table + Queue Loading

The `outboundDispatch.ts` currently works in-memory only (the SESSION_7E_LAUNCH_QUEUE is a hardcoded array). We need a real DB table.

### 4a. Migration: outbound_dispatch table

```sql
-- 20260315000005_outbound_dispatch_table.sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS outbound_dispatch (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  title TEXT NOT NULL,
  type TEXT NOT NULL, -- olive_branch, crown_letter, publication, etc.
  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft',
  priority TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'normal',
  recipient_name TEXT,
  recipient_org TEXT,
  recipient_contact TEXT,
  content_body TEXT NOT NULL,
  content_summary TEXT NOT NULL,
  channels TEXT[] NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  scheduled_for TIMESTAMPTZ,
  dispatched_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  created_by TEXT NOT NULL,
  reviewed_by UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  stamped_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  stamp_phrase TEXT,
  response_received_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  response_notes TEXT,
  follow_up_date TIMESTAMPTZ,
  campaign_id UUID,
  content_pipeline_id UUID,
  innovation_numbers INTEGER[] DEFAULT '{}',
  tags TEXT[] DEFAULT '{}',
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

ALTER TABLE outbound_dispatch ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

-- Only Founder (or admin) can see/manage dispatch queue
CREATE POLICY "Admin manages dispatch queue"
  ON outbound_dispatch
  FOR ALL
  USING (auth.uid() IN (
    SELECT id FROM auth.users WHERE raw_user_meta_data->>'role' = 'admin'
  ));

-- Insert initial items: Dead Internet article, Moo outreach, CFA outreach
INSERT INTO outbound_dispatch (title, type, status, priority, content_body, content_summary, channels, created_by, tags) VALUES
(
  'Dead Internet Defense — Medium Article',

  'draft',
  'high',
  'See BISHOP_DROPZONE/MEDIUM_DEAD_INTERNET_DEFENSE.md',
  'Medium-formatted article: Digg died because engagement was free. Four layers of bot defense built into LB economic DNA.',
  ARRAY['medium', 'cephas'],
  'bishop',
  ARRAY['dead-internet', 'security', 'medium', 'article']
),
(
  'Outreach — Moo Business Services (API Access Request)',
  'partnership_proposal',
  'draft',
  'high',
  'See BISHOP_DROPZONE/OUTREACH_MOO_BUSINESS_SERVICES.md',
  'API access request to Moo Business Services for Luxe stock variable-data QR business cards. Volume pricing and batch ordering.',
  ARRAY['email'],
  'bishop',
  ARRAY['moo', 'vendor', 'business-cards', 'outreach']
),
(
  'Outreach — Coins For Anything (Gear-Shaped QR Coin Quote)',
  'partnership_proposal',
  'draft',
  'high',
  'See BISHOP_DROPZONE/OUTREACH_COINS_FOR_ANYTHING.md',
  'Quote request for gear-shaped challenge coins with QR laser engraving. 100/500/1000 units. Veteran-owned vendor.',
  ARRAY['email'],
  'bishop',
  ARRAY['coins-for-anything', 'vendor', 'challenge-coins', 'outreach']
);
```

### 4b. Wire outboundDispatch.ts to use the DB table

Update the helper functions in `outboundDispatch.ts` to read/write from `outbound_dispatch` table instead of in-memory arrays. The existing function signatures stay the same — just swap the data source.

Key changes:
- `createOutboundDraft()` → INSERT into outbound_dispatch
- `submitForReview()` → UPDATE status = 'review'
- `stampItem()` → UPDATE status = 'stamped', stamped_at, stamp_phrase
- `queueForDispatch()` → UPDATE status = 'queued', scheduled_for
- `markDispatched()` → UPDATE status = 'dispatched', dispatched_at
- `getReadyForDispatch()` → SELECT WHERE status = 'stamped' OR (status = 'queued' AND scheduled_for <= NOW())
- Keep the SESSION_7E_LAUNCH_QUEUE as a seed migration (one-time insert)

## TASK 5: Print Order Service Wiring (Deferred from Session 23)

Connect `print_orders` and `print_batches` to the Printful edge function.

File: `platform/src/lib/services/printOrderService.ts`

```typescript
import { supabase } from '@/integrations/supabase/client';

export async function createPrintOrder(params: {
  userId: string;
  productionLevelId: string;
  quantity: number;
  designData: Record<string, unknown>;
  shippingAddress: Record<string, unknown>;
  creditsCost: number;
}) {
  const { data: order, error } = await supabase
    .from('print_orders')
    .insert({
      user_id: params.userId,
      production_level_id: params.productionLevelId,
      quantity: params.quantity,
      design_data: params.designData,
      shipping_address: params.shippingAddress,
      credits_cost: params.creditsCost,
      status: 'pending_approval',
      requires_approval: true,
    })
    .select()
    .single();

  if (error) throw error;
  return order;
}

export async function approvePrintOrder(orderId: string, approvedBy: string) {
  const { data: order, error } = await supabase
    .from('print_orders')
    .update({
      status: 'approved',
      approved_by: approvedBy,
      approved_at: new Date().toISOString(),
    })
    .eq('id', orderId)
    .select()
    .single();

  if (error) throw error;

  // After approval, create draft order via Printful
  const { data: printfulResult, error: pfError } = await supabase.functions.invoke('printful-api', {
    body: {
      action: 'create_order',
      data: {
        confirm: false, // DRAFT — Founder confirms separately
        order: {
          recipient: order.shipping_address,
          items: [{
            // Map from our design_data to Printful product/variant IDs
            variant_id: order.design_data.printful_variant_id,
            quantity: order.quantity,
            files: order.design_data.print_files || [],
          }],
        },
      },
    },
  });

  if (pfError) {
    // Mark order as failed, don't lose it
    await supabase
      .from('print_orders')
      .update({ status: 'printful_error', metadata: { error: pfError.message } })
      .eq('id', orderId);
    throw pfError;
  }

  // Store Printful order ID
  await supabase
    .from('print_orders')
    .update({
      status: 'printful_draft',
      metadata: { printful_order_id: printfulResult.result?.id },
    })
    .eq('id', orderId);

  return { order, printfulResult };
}

export async function confirmPrintfulOrder(orderId: string) {
  // Get the Printful order ID from our records
  const { data: order } = await supabase
    .from('print_orders')
    .select('metadata')
    .eq('id', orderId)
    .single();

  if (!order?.metadata?.printful_order_id) {
    throw new Error('No Printful order ID found');
  }

  // Confirm the order in Printful (sends to production)
  const { data, error } = await supabase.functions.invoke('printful-api', {
    body: {
      action: 'confirm_order',
      data: {
        order_id: order.metadata.printful_order_id,
      },
    },
  });

  if (error) throw error;

  await supabase
    .from('print_orders')
    .update({ status: 'in_production' })
    .eq('id', orderId);

  return data;
}

export async function getPendingOrders() {
  const { data, error } = await supabase
    .from('print_orders')
    .select('*, print_production_levels(*)')
    .eq('status', 'pending_approval')
    .order('created_at', { ascending: true });

  if (error) throw error;
  return data || [];
}
```

Also add `confirm_order` action to the Printful edge function (`printful-api/index.ts`):

```typescript
case 'confirm_order':
  endpoint = `https://api.printful.com/orders/${data.order_id}/confirm`;
  method = 'POST';
  break;
```

## TASK 6: useCanonicalStats() Component Migration

The `useCanonicalStats()` hook exists and the `platform_canonical` table has 14 rows (updated Session 23). Migrate components that still hardcode numbers.

**Search for these patterns and replace with hook calls:**

```bash
# Find hardcoded numbers
grep -rn "1,662\|1662" platform/src/
grep -rn "\"123\"\|123 Crown" platform/src/
grep -rn "7 provisional\|seven provisional" platform/src/
grep -rn "\$630,000\|\$525,000" platform/src/
```

**Priority files (based on Session 22 code review):**
- `HallOfInnovations.tsx` — innovation count
- `PatentPortfolio.tsx` — patent count, Crown Jewels count
- `ProfessionalLanding.tsx` — all stats in hero section
- `RedCarpet.tsx` — onboarding stats
- `IPPortfolioPage.tsx` — portfolio numbers (created Session 22)

**Pattern:**
```typescript
import { useCanonicalStats } from '@/hooks/useCanonicalStats';

// Inside component:
const { data: stats, isLoading } = useCanonicalStats();
// Then use: stats?.innovation_count, stats?.crown_jewels, stats?.patent_applications, etc.
// Fallback to hardcoded if loading: stats?.innovation_count ?? 1662
```
